2026-04-07T21:52:02.1482614Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m

If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}

2026-04-07T21:55:20.9570729Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T21:52:43.090228387Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57892,\"title\":\"[Azure AI Extensions OpenAI] Docs: Document expanded tool call tracing support in OpenTelemetry section\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57821 (commit 6990ac18d) by `@M-Hietala`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57821 expanded `OpenTelemetryResponseScope.cs` to emit trace spans and attributes for additional tool call types used in the OpenAI Responses API:\\n\\n- `code_interpreter_call` — captures `code`, `outputs`, `status`\\n- `file_search_call` — captures `queries`, `results`, `status`\\n- `web_search_call` — captures `action`, `status`\\n- `mcp_call` / `mcp_*` variants — captures `name`, `arguments`, `server_label`, `status`\\n\\nNew telemetry test files were also added: `ResponsesTelemetryTests.CodeInterpreter.cs`, `ResponsesTelemetryTests.FileSearch.cs`, `ResponsesTelemetryTests.Mcp.cs`, and `ResponsesTelemetryTests.WebSearch.cs`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG** (`CHANGELOG.md`): The `## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` \\u0026#34;Features Added\\u0026#34; section is empty. The expanded tool call tracing should be listed here.\\n- **README tracing section** (`README.md`): The existing \\u0026#34;Tracing\\u0026#34; section covers setup (enabling GenAI tracing, content recording, Azure Monitor / console exporters) but does not document *which tool types* are instrumented or *what span attributes* are captured per type. Users have no guidance on what trace data they can expect when using Code Interpreter, File Search, Web Search, or MCP tools.\\n- **No sample demonstrating tool tracing**: `tests/Samples/Sample_Responses_Tracing.cs` only shows a simple text-prompt trace. There is no snippet showing how tool-call spans appear in traces, which the README could link to.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Expanded OpenTelemetry tracing support to additional tool call types in the Responses API:\\n  - `code_interpreter_call` — traces code execution with `code`, `outputs`, and `status` fields (when content recording is enabled)\\n  - `file_search_call` — traces file search operations with `queries`, `results`, and `status` fields (when content recording is enabled)\\n  - `web_search_call` — traces web search calls with `action` and `status` fields (when content recording is enabled)\\n  - `mcp_call` and `mcp_*` variants — traces MCP tool calls with `name`, `arguments`, `server_label`, and `status` fields (when content recording is enabled)\\n  - Generic fallback tracing for any unrecognized `*_call` output item types\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/README.md`\\n\\nAdd a new subsection **\\u0026#34;Traced tool types\\u0026#34;** inside the existing `### Tracing` section, after the \\u0026#34;Enabling content recording\\u0026#34; subsection and before `## Troubleshooting`. Insert:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Traced tool types\\n\\nWhen GenAI tracing is enabled, the SDK emits a `gen_ai.tool.message` event on the response span for each tool call present in the response. The following tool types are supported:\\n\\n| Tool type | Always captured | Captured when content recording is enabled |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `function_call` | call ID | function name, arguments |\\n| `code_interpreter_call` | call ID, status | code, outputs |\\n| `file_search_call` | call ID, status | queries, results |\\n| `web_search_call` | call ID, status | action |\\n| `mcp_call` | call ID, server label, status | name, arguments |\\n| `mcp_*` (other MCP variants) | call ID, server label, status | name, arguments, input, output |\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Binary data such as images or generated files is never captured in traces regardless of content recording settings.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new README snippet is strictly required for the CHANGELOG fix. However, optionally add a snippet demonstrating a response that triggers tool calls, to show what appears in traces. If added:\\n\\n1. Add a test method in `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/tests/Samples/Sample_Responses_Tracing.cs` wrapping tool-call tracing setup with `#region Snippet:Sample_ResponsesToolCallTracing` / `#endregion`.\\n2. Insert a ```` ```C# Snippet:Sample_ResponsesToolCallTracing` ```` placeholder in the README after the new \\u0026#34;Traced tool types\\u0026#34; table.\\n\\nThis is optional — the table in the README already describes what is traced without requiring a live code sample.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections:\\n\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nOnly the content within the \\u0026#34;Tracing\\u0026#34; subsection and the CHANGELOG need updating.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — only needed if a new snippet was added in Step 2\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-07T21:55:20.9748847Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T21:52:58.448750617Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":81,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4090517017,\"number\":57183,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization\",\"body\":\"## Bug description\\nWhen using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.\\n\\nThe exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:\\n`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`\\n\\n## Package and environment\\n- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`\\n- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents\\n- OS: Windows\\n- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\nAfter the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\nThe full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.\\n\\n## Reproduction notes\\n1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.\\n2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.\\n3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.\\n4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.\\n\\n## Minimal observed output\\n```text\\nassistant(stream)\\u003e \\u003cfull answer omitted\\u003e\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent\\nUnhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel\\u003cAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall\\u003e.Write(...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\n```\\n\\n## Suspected root cause\\nThe generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.\\n\\nRelevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`\\n\\n## Impact\\n- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.\\n- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.\\n- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.\\n\\n## Workaround currently used\\nApplication-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.\\n\\n## Request\\nPlease confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute
